Sarah at Optus went quiet after our call 3 weeks ago. Short follow-up, don't pitch, just reopen the door.
Draft: Sarah Chen, Optus:
Hi Sarah,

It's been a few weeks since we spoke about the Q3 shortlist. Hope the planning is going well on your end.

Wanted to check in... not to push anything, just to see how things are landing internally.

Worth a quick 15 minutes? Here's my calendar link to book a time.

Baz
119 words · No pitch · Opens a door
Send it?
Yes and set a calendar reminder for another follow up in 2 days.
Sent. Calendar reminder set for Thursday, "Follow up Sarah Chen / Optus if no reply."

Logged to CRM · Reminder queued

One OS
online
LinkedIn post, the moment I realised most AI tools are built for demos, not the actual work. Sharp, under 200 words, in my voice. Here's a reel that was the inspiration: instagram.com/reel/example
Draft: 187 words:
Every AI demo I've seen is immaculate.

Clean interface. Instant response. Perfectly chosen use case.

Then you try to use it on Tuesday morning... three client fires, a contractor who hasn't updated their scope, an invoice 40 days overdue.

Suddenly the AI needs you to explain your whole business again.
It gives you a template.
You spend 20 minutes making the template fit.

That's not assistance. That's a better Google Doc.

The problem isn't the model. It's that most tools are built for the pitch, not the work.

What's the biggest gap between the AI demo and what actually helps you on a normal Tuesday?
Hook → tension → question · In your voice · Based on your last 8 posts
Queue it? Post it?
Post it. Also write up a long form article for the customer newsletter, last 7 days in our industry, search Perplexity and include sources.
Post queued for publish.

Searching Perplexity for the last 7 days in AI infrastructure and founder ops...

Newsletter draft: ready: 680 words, 4 sources cited, formatted for Substack. Topics: Claude 4 release signals, Perplexity funding, AI agent security concerns, and one founder ops case study.

Sources linked inline · Saved to vault/content/newsletter/
Review draft →
⚡
One OS
online
Update Henderson on the phase wrap, two weeks ahead of schedule, budget on track, one scope query to close.
Draft: Henderson account:
Hi Michael,

Quick update, we're tracking two weeks ahead of schedule heading into close, which is a good position to be in.

Budget is where it should be. No surprises.

One thing to sort before we wrap: a scope question on the data migration timeline. Nothing urgent, but I'd rather close it now than have it slow handover. Can we grab 20 minutes? Here's my calendar link to book a time.

Good momentum on this one.
Confident · No over-promising · Flags the issue cleanly · Not a status report
Send it?
Send it and draft up a handover brief that has potential timelines and a wrap up agreement.
Sent.

Handover brief: Henderson Group:
  • Phase close: 2 weeks ahead of schedule
  • Data migration scope query: resolve by 9 May
  • Handover window: 12–16 May (pending confirmation)
  • Wrap-up agreement: sign-off checklist + final deliverables log
Saved to vault/clients/henderson/ · Ready to send as PDF
Send brief →

  • Share onboarding deck with Michael before Friday

⚠ Needs sign-off: Phase 2 scope change, not yet approved by CFO.
Send follow-up notes to the team?
Yes. Send everyone their own items only. And book a 20-minute data migration call with their IT lead this week.
Follow-ups sent... each person received only their items.

Chase CFO approval now?

Back in the office

Your mission control for your business operating system.

Business OS Dashboard: Agents
3 step lifecycle

Set up in a day. Run 100k's an hour.

Day 1
Setup + First JTBD Live
In one guided session, your system is live. Accounts connected, first workflow running, you're talking to your OS on Telegram by the end of the call.
Days 2–30
Support + Tune
30 days of hands-on support. Daily check-ins and weekly calls to calibrate workflows, handle edge cases, and tighten the system around real usage.
Month 2+
Retainer or Run Solo
You own the system. Base retainer covers skills tuning, priority support, audits, and a monthly check-in. Additional workflows scoped and quoted separately.
🖥
Your computer. Your data.
Runs on your Mac. Nothing goes to a cloud you don't control. Your jobs, your clients, your numbers — on your machine.
🤝
Baz sets it up with you.
You don't touch a line of code. One session, system is live. You just use it.
🎯
Built for your work. Not every business.
Set up around the jobs costing you the most time and money. Not a generic feature list you have to configure yourself.
What you get out of the box

Everything you need. Nothing you don't.

💬
Telegram interface
Full AI on your phone. Text, voice, photos, documents. Send a voice note, get a business insight back.
🧠
Persistent memory
Remembers your business across every conversation. Context carries forward, nothing needs re-explaining.
👥
Multi-agent team
Specialist agents for finance, comms, research, content, and ops running in parallel, reporting to you.
🕐
Scheduled automations
Morning standups, daily reconciliations, pipeline checks, EOD reports. Set once, run forever.
📊
Live dashboard
See what every agent is doing, what's scheduled, what it costs, and what it remembers in one view.
🔒
Your data. Your machine.
Everything runs locally. SQLite database, local files, no cloud. Kill switch to stop everything remotely.

Playbooks

The business operating system that keeps on giving

Initial setup builds the foundation. Every month on retainer, workflows are assessed, new skills are scoped, built, and activated around what your business actually needs. Month 1 might be pipeline. Month 3 might be ad health. Month 6 might be full finance automation. The catalog grows and customised to you.

🔭
Lead Pipeline Visibility
Know what's quoted, what's pending, and where next month's revenue comes from.
Browser-based quote tracker, no Airtable, no Monday, no subscription. Shows every open quote, when it was sent, and when to follow up. Auto follow-up fires on quotes not responded to in 5 days.

Lead source tagging shows which channels are bringing paying jobs. Pipeline goes from invisible to predictable. The whole thing lives in your vault, owned by you permanently.
Runs on Business OS HTML Builder Gmail API Browser localStorage
📊
Meta / Google Ads Health
Daily performance brief across both platforms, before you open a dashboard.
Pulls Meta and Google Ads data via native CLI, no logins, no dashboards to check manually. Surfaces spend, CPL, conversions, and anomalies every morning to Telegram.

Auto-generates a weekly recommendations card: top performers, budget reallocation, negative keywords to add, ad schedule to tighten. Estimated recovery: 20–40% of wasted spend redirected to real leads.
Runs on Meta Ads CLI Google Ads API Business OS Scheduler Google Cloud Project (free setup)
📅
Month-End Closer
Compresses a multi-day close into an automated run.
Executes closing checklists, prepares journal entries, generates closing reports. Connects to Xero or MYOB, reconciles transactions, flags exceptions, and generates a board-ready summary.

The finance agent handles the pattern work. You review the exceptions. What used to take 2–3 days of back-and-forth happens overnight.
Runs on GL Reconciler Agent Business OS Scheduler Xero API or MYOB API (free setup)
⭐
Automated Review Generation
Every completed job or transaction becomes a review request. Automatically.
Invoice paid → personalised request to the customer 3–5 days later, using their name, job type, and context. No reply after 7 days? One polite follow-up. Sequence stops the moment they leave a review.

Monthly report shows review velocity and which job types convert best. No more relying on anyone to remember to ask.
Runs on Gmail API Business OS Scheduler Google Business Profile API (free)
✍️
Content on Autopilot
Queue it once. The OS schedules, publishes, and repurposes across channels.
Share a photo, a voice note, or a rough idea via Telegram. The content agent drafts a post in your voice. Approve with a thumbs up and it schedules and publishes. No briefs. No copy sessions.

Monthly content calendar built automatically from your work history, your reviews, and what's performing. Consistent presence without consistent effort.
Runs on Telegram Bot Claude Content Agent Google Business Profile API Blotato or Buffer (~$20/mo)
🔁

Why Baz on retainer compounds

The setup gets you running. The retainer is where the real value builds. Each month we scope what's costing you the most in your workflows, identify the highest-leverage automations, and prioritise the build roadmap. New capabilities get scoped and quoted separately. You don't pay to maintain a static tool. You pay to keep the system evolving with your business. Most clients see their third month cover the entire year's cost.
